Intelligence Briefing
Welcome to the World Beneath Neon Town
Your Story

You were once a low-level courier for the syndicates that run this neon-soaked town. One bad deal later, you're left with nothing — no crew, no cash, no reputation. The bosses have written you off. The streets have forgotten your name.

But you're not done. Starting from the bottom with nothing but nerve and quick hands, you'll work your way back up — one stolen car, one score, one close call at a time. Build your bankroll. Prove you can't be erased.

The Town

The town never sleeps and it never forgives. Neon towers cast long shadows over gridlocked streets crawling with Police, Brave Citizens who won't look the other way, Scared Citizens too afraid to speak up, and the lurking Mobster Boss who answers to no one but his own vendetta. Every block is a gamble — every alley, a potential escape route or dead end.

A Garage in the northwest district is your only safe haven. Deliver stolen vehicles there to cash in — and as long as you remain within the Garage zone, you cannot be harmed by anything: bullets, police melee, and vehicle impacts are all blocked while inside. The rest of the map? That's hostile territory.

Spawning Rules
  • 150 citizens are spawned at game-start, distributed randomly across walkable tiles. Each citizen is assigned a class using a fixed ratio: 10 % Police, 30 % Brave Citizen, 60 % Scared Citizen. One additional citizen is spawned as the Mobster Boss.
  • ~40 parked vehicles and additional moving vehicles populate the roads at start. Moving cars always carry a driver whose class matches the citizen ratio above. Parked cars have a 50/50 chance of carrying an occupant. 10 % of vehicles are Police cruisers (blue with sirens); the rest are civilian cars.
  • Police reinforcements are not pre-spawned — they are summoned dynamically when a crime is witnessed. For your crimes: a Police witness dispatches wanted × 2 units (minimum 2); a Brave Citizen witness adds one unit if below that cap. The Mobster Boss's crimes trigger the same system independently using his own wanted level (mobsterWanted × 2 cap) — and every cop he shoots dispatches reinforcements on the spot, bypassing the witness check entirely. Reinforcements spawn 600–1000 px away from the player at a random angle.
  • Cash pickups — 40 glowing icons ($25 / $50 / $100 / $250) — are placed on road tiles at game-start. The pool is kept topped up: whenever fewer than 15 remain on the map a new pickup is spawned on a random road tile.
  • Medicine bottles are placed on walkable tiles at game-start and never respawn once consumed: 30 × Green Medicine Bottles (5 HP) and 10 × Blue Medicine Bottles (10 HP). Both you and the Mobster Boss can pick them up — first to reach a bottle takes it.
  • Mission 8 override — during the 60-second survival mission, all Police switch to town-wide chase mode regardless of distance. No additional reinforcement spawns are triggered; the existing pool simply becomes permanently active.
Crime & Witness System
  • Crimes are only reported if Police or Brave Citizens witness them nearby — Scared Citizens will never report you.
  • Stealing a parked car goes unreported if no Police or Brave Citizens are watching.
  • Stealing from a Brave Citizen driver is always reported — they witness it directly as they are ejected.
  • Stealing a Police car with a driver is always reported; a parked police car only triggers a report if Police or Brave Citizens are nearby at the moment of theft.
  • Attacking Police escalates your wanted level only if witnessed — killing an officer always escalates further as the dying cop radios it in.
  • The higher the active wanted level — yours or the Mobster Boss's, whichever is greater — the faster, more aggressive, and more trigger-happy the Police become, and the more reinforcements are dispatched.
  • Driving a stolen Police car near any Police within 250 units continuously triggers a report — Brave Citizens cannot report you for this.
  • Two-tier chase — Police actively chase any wanted target — you or the Mobster Boss — within 250 units. Once in range they lock on. After both targets leave range they pursue for a 15-second grace period before standing down. During Mission 8, all Police chase town-wide.
  • Wanted decay — stop shooting and stop committing witnessed crimes. Your wanted level decays only when you are not shooting and no reinforcements have been dispatched in the last 15 seconds. Once wanted hits zero, all Police stand down and despawn. The Mobster Boss's wanted level decays on its own independent 15-second timer — even when your wanted level is zero.
  • Patrol cops do not shoot — Police only open fire when actively chasing. Cops outside chase range patrol roads silently.
  • Collect cash scattered across the town and earn bounties for completing missions.
Damage, Health & Medicine
  • All parties — you, Police, Brave Citizens, Scared Citizens, and the Mobster Boss — each have 100 HP.
  • Your bullets deal 10 HP to the Mobster Boss (10 shots to kill), 20 HP to Police (5 shots), 50 HP to citizens (2 shots).
  • Police bullets deal 10 HP to you (10 shots to kill) and 20 HP to the Mobster Boss — use this against him (5 shots to kill).
  • Mobster bullets deal 10 HP to you, 20 HP to Police, and 50 HP to citizens.
  • Vehicle impact (speed > 30) kills citizens instantly; deals 10 HP per hit to the Mobster Boss; a police car ramming you on foot also deals 10 HP per hit.
  • Police melee — a Police officer closing to melee range strikes for 10 HP per hit (once every 0.8 seconds). The Garage safe zone blocks this too.
  • If your health drops to zero, it is game over.
  • Find Green Medicine Bottles (5 HP, 30 across the map) and rarer Blue Medicine Bottles (10 HP, 10 across the map) scattered across the town. Both you and the Mobster Boss can pick them up.
  • Medicine only works if you are injured — no effect at full health. The bottle stays on the ground until used. Once consumed it is gone for good.
  • The Garage is a safe zone — no bullet damage can reach you while you are inside it.
The Mobster Boss
  • The Mobster Boss recognises you within 250 units, chases you, and shoots within 300 units — he deals 10 HP per bullet to you and has 100 HP.
  • He stays hostile while you are in range. After 15 seconds out of range he stands down and reverts to a Scared Citizen — fleeing you and never reporting crimes.
  • The Mobster Boss is subject to exactly the same crime rules as you — Police and Brave Citizens witness his crimes under identical conditions, his wanted level escalates the same way yours does, and Police reinforcements are dispatched for his crimes just as they are for yours.
  • Every cop he shoots raises his wanted level by +1 immediately — this resets his 15-second decay timer and dispatches additional Police reinforcements on the spot, bypassing the witness check entirely. His wanted level is capped at 5 stars.
  • Brave Citizens report his crimes; Scared Citizens flee him but never report — identical to how they treat you.
  • His wanted level and yours are completely independent — each builds and decays on its own 15-second timer, regardless of whether you have a wanted level. When his wanted level reaches zero, Police units that were chasing him immediately stand down.
  • Police dynamically prioritise — if his wanted level is active and he is closer to a cop than you are, that cop switches its chase target to him. The moment you become the closer (or only) wanted target, cops switch back to chasing you.
  • His priority is always to kill you. He will only evade when a Police unit is actively chasing him and within 200 units — even then he blends his retreat with continued pursuit of you.
  • Tip — lure him near active Police: every police bullet deals 20 HP to him. Shoot cops while he is nearby to spike his wanted level instantly and draw reinforcements straight onto him.
Your Missions
1
Steal a car and deliver it to the Garage.
Walk up to any civilian car and press E to steal it. Drive it to the Garage in the northwest and park inside the zone.
2
Collect $500 in cash pickups.
Glowing cash icons ($25, $50, $100, $250) are scattered across the town. Walk or drive over them. Your total earned cash must reach $500.
3
Steal a Police car and deliver it to the Garage.
Find a Police car (blue with sirens), press E to hijack it, and drive it to the Garage. If the car has a driver, stealing it always raises your wanted level. If it's parked and unattended, it only triggers a report if Police or Brave Citizens are nearby. Once driving, any Police that spots you in the stolen car will report it immediately.
4
Escape the Police — bring your wanted level back to zero.
Stop committing crimes and stop shooting. Wanted decay requires both: not shooting and no new police dispatches for 15 seconds. Every witnessed crime resets the timer. Once your wanted level hits zero, all Police stand down and the mission completes.
5
Reach a 3-star wanted level.
Commit crimes near Police or Brave Citizens to raise your wanted level. Shoot, steal cars, or run people over while witnesses are present.
6
Collect $4,000 in total cash.
Keep picking up cash scattered around the town. This counts all cash earned since game start, including mission rewards. Rewards alone won't cover this — you'll need roughly $2,000 in street cash on top of mission payouts.
7
Terminate the Mobster Boss.
Hunt down the Mobster Boss. He has 100 HP — 10 of your bullets to finish him. He shoots back at 10 damage per hit and can heal with medicine bottles. Tip — lure him near active Police, commit crimes near witnesses to raise his wanted level and let the police help finish him off.
8
Survive 60 seconds at 4-star wanted level.
Raise your wanted level to 4 stars, then stay alive for 60 seconds. All Police chase town-wide. The survival timer pauses inside the Garage. If your wanted level drops below 4, the timer resets.
